title: Multidimensional C-style Arrays

exercises.title: Row Sum Calculator
exercises.prompt_md:
## Goal
Read a 3x4 matrix of integers and print the sum of each row.

## Input
- 3 lines, each containing exactly 4 space-separated integers (one row per line).

## Output
- 3 lines, each containing the sum of the corresponding input row.

## Requirements
1. Declare a 2D C-style array `int matrix[3][4]` -- do not use `std::array` or `std::vector`.
2. Use nested `for` loops with `std::size_t` loop variables to read the input.
3. For each row, compute the sum using an inner loop and print it on its own line.
4. Do not hard-code the row or column counts in loop conditions -- use `constexpr std::size_t` variables named `rows` and `cols`.
5. Include `<iostream>` and `<cstddef>`.

exercises.title: Matrix Column Maximum
exercises.prompt_md:
## Goal
Read a 3x5 matrix of integers and find the maximum value in each column.

## Input
- 3 lines, each containing exactly 5 space-separated integers.

## Output
- A single line containing 5 space-separated integers: the maximum value from each column (column 0 through column 4), separated by single spaces.

## Requirements
1. Declare `int matrix[3][5]` using `constexpr std::size_t` variables for the dimensions.
2. Read the matrix using nested `for` loops.
3. For each column, iterate over all rows to find the maximum value. Initialize your running maximum with the value in row 0 of that column.
4. Print all 5 column maxima on one line, separated by spaces, followed by a newline.
5. Do not use any standard library algorithm functions -- use only loops and comparisons.
6. Include `<iostream>` and `<cstddef>`.
